heading: (intro)
raw: Directed by Victor Fleming. Written by Noel Langley, Florence Ryerson, and Edgar Allan Woolf, based on the novel The Wonderful Wizard of Oz by L. Frank Baum. Music by Harold Arlen, Lyrics by Yip Harburg.
clean: Directed by Victor Fleming. Written by Noel Langley, Florence Ryerson, and Edgar Allan Woolf, based on the novel The Wonderful Wizard of Oz by L. Frank Baum. Music by Harold Arlen, Lyrics by Yip Harburg.

heading: Dorothy
raw: Toto, I've a feeling we're not in Kansas anymore. We must be over the rainbow! [Note: This line is ranked #4 in the American Film Institute's list of the top 100 movie quotations in American cinema].
clean: Toto, I've a feeling we're not in Kansas anymore. We must be over the rainbow! .

heading: Dorothy
raw: Well, I... I think that it... that it wasn't enough to just want to see Uncle Henry and Auntie Em... and it's that if I ever go looking for my heart's desire again, I won't look any further than my own backyard; because if it isn't there, I never really lost it to begin with.
clean: Well, I... I think that it... that it wasn't enough to just want to see Uncle Henry and Auntie Em... and it's that if I ever go looking for my heart's desire again, I won't look any further than my own backyard; because if it isn't there, I never really lost it to begin with.

heading: Dorothy
raw: There's no place like home; there's no place like home; there's no place like home... [Note: This line is ranked #23 in the American Film Institute's list of the top 100 movie quotations in American cinema].
clean: There's no place like home; there's no place like home; there's no place like home... .

heading: Dorothy
raw: [last lines] Oh, but anyway, Toto, we're home – home! And this is my room – and you're all here – and I'm not going to leave here ever, ever again, because I love you all! And... oh, Auntie Em, there's no place like home!
clean: Oh, but anyway, Toto, we're home - home! And this is my room - and you're all here - and I'm not going to leave here ever, ever again, because I love you all! And... oh, Auntie Em, there's no place like home!

heading: Scarecrow
raw: Why, if I had a brain I could...[singing] I could while away the hours,Conferrin' with the flowers,Consultin' with the rain.And my head I'd be scratchin'While my thoughts were busy hatchin'If I only had a brain.
clean: Why, if I had a brain I could... I could while away the hours,Conferrin' with the flowers,Consultin' with the rain.And my head I'd be scratchin'While my thoughts were busy hatchin'If I only had a brain.

heading: Tin Man

clean: When a man's an empty kettle,He should be on his mettle,And yet I'm torn apart.Just because I'm presumin'That I could be kinda humanIf I only had a heart.

heading: Cowardly Lion
raw: Put 'em up, put 'em up! Which one of ya first?
clean: Put 'em up, put 'em up! Which one of ya first?

heading: Cowardly Lion
raw: I’ll fight you both together if you want! I’ll fight you with one paw tied behind my back! I’ll fight you standin’ on one foot! I’ll fight you wit’ my eyes closed!
clean: I'll fight you both together if you want! I'll fight you with one paw tied behind my back! I'll fight you standin' on one foot! I'll fight you wit' my eyes closed!

heading: Cowardly Lion

clean: Yeah, it's sad, believe me, Missy,When you're born to be the sissy,Without the vim and verve.But I could show my prowess,Be the lion, not the "mouse,"If I only had the nerve.

heading: Cowardly Lion

clean: Each rabbit would show respect to me/The chipmunks genuflect to me/Tho' my tail would lash/I would show compash/For ev'ry underling/If I, if I were king/Just king.

heading: Cowardly Lion
raw: Read what my medal says. Courage! Ain't it the truth! ain't it the truth!
clean: Read what my medal says. Courage! Ain't it the truth! ain't it the truth!

heading: Cowardly Lion
raw: In another hour, I'll be king of the forest! LONG LIVE THE KING!
clean: In another hour, I'll be king of the forest! LONG LIVE THE KING!

heading: Wicked Witch of the West
raw: [to Glinda] Very well – I'll bide my time. [to Dorothy] And as for you, my fine lady, it's true I can't attend to you here and now as I'd like; but just try to stay out of my way – just try! I'll get you, my pretty, and your little dog too! [With a burst of laughter, she whirls around and vanishes in a burst of smoke and fire and a clap of thunder.] [Note: Bolded line is ranked #99 in the American Film Institute's list of the top 100 movie quotations in American cinema].
clean: Very well - I'll bide my time. And as for you, my fine lady, it's true I can't attend to you here and now as I'd like; but just try to stay out of my way - just try! I'll get you, my pretty, and your little dog too! .

heading: Wicked Witch of the West

clean: That's a good little girl. I knew you'd see reason. Aah! Ohhh!

heading: Wicked Witch of the West

heading: The Wizard

heading: The Wizard

heading: The Wizard
raw: [to the Tin Man] Back where I come from, there are men who do nothing all day but good deeds. And their hearts are no bigger than yours is! But they have one thing you haven't got: a testimonial. […] A heart is not judged by how much you love; but by how much you are loved by others.

heading: Glinda

heading: Aunt Em
raw: Almira Gulch! Just because you own half the county doesn't mean that you have the power to run the rest of us. For 23 years, I've been dying to tell you what I thought of you, and now... well, being a Christian woman, I can't say it!

clean: I think I'm going to miss you most of all.

heading: Quotes about The Wizard of Oz
raw: It's a wonderful universal story. I mean you look at the Wizard of Oz — it takes a reality which is the beginning of the movie, and turns it into a mythology. … You know all great story telling is a form of myth. Caleb Deschanel, These Amazing Shadows (2011)
clean: It's a wonderful universal story. I mean you look at the Wizard of Oz - it takes a reality which is the beginning of the movie, and turns it into a mythology. ... You know all great story telling is a form of myth. Caleb Deschanel, These Amazing Shadows (2011)

heading: Quotes about The Wizard of Oz
raw: I would watch Wizard of Oz, like every day, when I was two. I had a hard time understanding that I couldn't go into the film, because it felt so real to me. Zooey Deschanel, These Amazing Shadows (2011)
